mxCell.value: Pre-Call Intelligence Engine — BPWealth Voice Agent
How client data is assembled and used to personalise every call — 30 seconds before dialling
DATA SOURCES

Demographics & KYC
Age · Location · Risk profile · Language

Trading Behaviour
Segments · Ticket size · P&L · Activity

Portfolio Holdings
Holdings · Sector · Cash balance

StoxCall History
Tips · Conversion rate · Outcomes

App Behaviour
Watchlist · Screens · Sessions

Campaign Engagement
WhatsApp · Email · Call CTR

Service & Interaction
Complaints · RM notes · Dispositions
PRE-CALL INTELLIGENCE ENGINE (T−30s)

Pre-Call Intelligence Engine

Assembles full client context · Runs eligibility filters · Selects optimal scrip · Builds personalisation hooks · Sets call controls
SIGNAL FLOW TIMELINE

T−30s: Data Fetch

• Pull CRM: risk, language, history
• Pull trading platform: holdings, cash
• Pull app events: watchlist, sessions
• Pull StoxCall: tips, objection history

T−10s: Eligibility Filter

• Segment check (Cash / F&O eligible?)
• Risk profile filter (block penny stocks)
• Sector over-exposure check
• Suppression flags (DND, complaint)

T−5s: Scrip Selection

• Match scrip to watchlist activity
• Calibrate ticket to cash + history
• Sector diversification check
• Set entry / target / stop-loss

T−2s: Hook Assembly

• Watchlist hook (primary opener)
• Past win reference for confidence
• Holding complement angle
• Pre-load objection rebuttals

T=0: Call Begins

• Opens in preferred language
• Tone calibrated to RM notes
• Max 5 turns set
• Escalation trigger armed
CONTEXT PACKET — LOADED INTO AGENT MEMORY

Client Identity
Name · ID · Language
Risk profile · Segments

Portfolio Snapshot
Cash · Holdings
Sector exposure

Recommendation
Scrip · Entry · Target
Stop-loss · Qty

Personalisation Hooks
Watchlist · Past win
Complement · Objection

Call Controls
Suppress flags · Max turns
Escalation trigger

Training Signal
A/B variant · Conv. rate
Objection tag
context packet
Data Sources → Signals → Call Behaviour
Each data source extracts specific signals that drive specific agent behaviour during the call
Demographics & KYC
Age 38 · Self-employed
Income ₹25–50L / year
Risk: Moderate-aggressive
Language: Gujarati preferred
→ Shorter pitch · flexible window
→ Ticket size anchored at ₹1–2L
→ Allow cash + F&O · block penny stocks
→ Open call in Hinglish / Gujarati blend
Trading Behaviour
Segments: Cash + F&O
Avg ticket: ₹85,000
60% intraday · 40% swing
Conversion rate: 62%
→ Can pitch F&O — full product range unlocked
→ Suggest 150 shares @ ₹820 = ₹1.23L
→ Lead with intraday framing every call
→ High priority in dial queue (above avg)
Portfolio Holdings
Holds: HDFC · Infosys · Tata Motors
IT sector over-exposed (42%)
Cash available: ₹2,40,000
→ Tata Motors held — pitch as add-on position
→ Suppress IT stocks; auto/banking adds diversification
→ 150 shares @ ₹820 fits within available cash
App Behaviour
Watchlist: Tata Motors (2 days ago)
Research note opened (1 day ago)
Drop-off: exits at order placement
→ Primary hook: 'I noticed you were tracking this...'
→ Warm lead — validate their own research
→ CTA bypasses order screen via WhatsApp deep link
DATA SOURCE
SIGNALS EXTRACTED
HOW USED IN THE CALL
Live Call Personalisation Hooks
How each data signal translates into a specific spoken line or agent behaviour during the call
SIGNAL (DATA)
AGENT SAYS
WHY IT WORKS

Watchlist signal
Tata Motors added
2 days ago
"Rajeshbhai, I noticed you were looking at Tata Motors on the app two days ago — and our research team has put out a specific note on it today. It will only take 60 seconds."
Makes it feel like a direct response to something the client was already thinking — not a cold call.

Past win reference
HDFC Bank call
+8.2% in March
"You did really well on the HDFC Bank call we shared in March — similar setup here: tight stop-loss, clear catalyst, short time horizon. That is what makes this compelling."
References a specific, real outcome the client experienced — far more credible than generic claims about track record.

Current holding
Client already holds
Tata Motors

"You are already in Tata Motors, so you know the story. This is an opportunity to add to a position you already believe in, at what our team thinks is a strong re-entry point before the EV data drops next week."
Reframes from 'buy something new' (risky) to 'add to something you own' (familiar). Reduces perceived risk.

Cash + ticket size
₹2.4L cash · ticket ₹85K
→ suggest 150 shares
"At today's price of ₹820, 150 shares is ₹1.23 lakhs — that is within your normal range and leaves you with comfortable margin. You are not stretching at all for this one."
Specific numbers calibrated to this client eliminate the 'I need to check my cash' objection before it is raised.

Objection pattern
Historically raises
volatility concern

"I completely understand — this market feels unpredictable. But the stop-loss is at ₹805, so maximum downside on 150 shares is ₹2,250. The upside to target is ₹9,000. The asymmetry is firmly in your favour."
Rebuttal is pre-loaded from client's objection history. Response is fast, specific, and data-backed — not generic.

Loss history
Midcap IT call -3.1%
6 weeks ago

"I am aware the midcap IT call a few weeks ago did not go the way we wanted — I will not pretend otherwise. What is different here: different sector, dated catalyst, not a macro view. I would not call if I were not confident."
Acknowledging the loss before the client raises it builds credibility. Suppressing it destroys trust.

Language preference
Gujarati registered
Hinglish in past calls
Opens in Hinglish: "Namaste Rajeshbhai, aaj ek specific opportunity hai Tata Motors mein..." Switches on client signal: "Tamne Gujarati maa vaato kari sakhu? Bilkul, mari pan suvida che."
Starting in Hinglish (natural blend) feels more comfortable. Switching on request shows respect, builds rapport.

App drop-off pattern
Exits at order
placement screen

"If you would like to proceed, I will send you a WhatsApp right now with entry, target, stop-loss, and a link. Tap it and your dealer has everything — you do not have to navigate to the order screen yourself."
App data shows hesitation at order placement. The CTA bypasses that friction point via WhatsApp deep link.
Personalisation Tiers — Pre-call · Live Call · Post-call
Client: Rajesh Mehta · BPW-40291 | Language: Gujarati/Hinglish | Risk: Moderate-aggressive | Conversion: 62% | Cash: ₹2,40,000 | Status: Active
TIER 1 — PRE-CALL (T−30s)
Pull risk profile, holdings, cash, app activity from CRM + data lake
Run eligibility filter — only pitch segments client is cleared for
Select scrip matching risk appetite and available cash
Detect preferred language from CRM + app + past call notes
Check suppression flags: DND, open complaint, recent loss
Pre-load top 3 objection rebuttals based on client's objection history
Sources: CRM · Data lake · Trading platform · RMS
TIER 2 — LIVE CALL (real-time)
Open with personalised hook: watchlist, past win, or market event
Reference current holding — position tip as complementary, not competing
Deliver exact ticket size calibrated to cash and typical position size
Switch language instantly if client signals preference mid-call
Personalised objection rebuttal using client's own history
Sentiment monitor in parallel — escalate on 2× negative signal
Tools: Context packet · LangGraph state · Redis session memory
TIER 3 — POST-CALL (feedback loop)
Log outcome (converted / callback / declined) to CRM
Tag objection type — updates client objection profile for next call
Update StoxCall participation: tip outcome, acted vs ignored
Send WhatsApp: entry, target, SL, research note, callback confirmation
Feed transcript + outcome into A/B test framework
Re-score client conversion probability for next recommendation
Tools: CRM write · WhatsApp API · Kafka event stream
feeds into
feeds into
next call is smarter
Fine-tuning from 12+ months of call recordings
Step 1: Transcribe all recordings (Whisper / Deepgram batch)
Step 2: Label by outcome + objection type per call
Step 3: LoRA fine-tune on objection → rebuttal pairs (Llama 3.1 8B)
Step 4: Shadow mode eval vs human callers · target 67% conv. rate
